post.description:
Generates a mail and sends it to the customer. The mail content is rendered with data passed through the request.

Take a look at the `salesChannel` entity for possible values. For example `{{ salesChannel.name }}` can be used.

recipients.description: A map of recipients with name and mail address.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

salesChannelId.description: Identifier of the sales channel from which the mail should be send.

contentHtml.description: The content of the mail in HTML format.

contentPlain.description: The content of the mail as plain text.

senderEmail.description: Mail address of the sender. If not set, `core.basicInformation.email` or `core.mailerSettings.senderAddress` will be used from the shop configuration.

mediaIds.description: List of media identifiers which should be attached to the mail.

documentIds.description: List of document identifiers which should be attached to the mail.

binAttachments.description: A list of binary attachments which should be added to the mail.

recipientsBcc.description: A mail address or a map of recipients with name and mail address to be set in BCC.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

recipientsCc.description: A mail address or a map of recipients with name and mail address to be set in CC.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

replyTo.description: A mail address or a map of mail addresses with name to be set in reply-to.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

returnPath.description: A mail address or a map of mail addresses with name to be set in return-path.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

mailTemplateId.description: Identifier of the mail template that should be used for attachments and sender defaults.

mailTemplateData.description: A map of available data that is used to fill the mail template. Values in this map override generated or entity-derived template data with the same top-level keys.

testMode.description: Enables mail test mode. The mail is still sent, but template rendering runs with the renderer's test mode enabled, a fallback `order.deepLinkCode` of `home` is injected when missing, and debug headers such as `X-Shopware-Language-Id` plus available event or sales-channel headers are added to the message.

post.description: Validates if content for a mail can be rendered without sending an email.

contentHtml.description: The content of the mail in HTML format.

contentPlain.description: The content of the mail as plain text.

204.description: Returns a no content response indicating the mail content was rendered successfully.

post.description: Generates a preview of a mail template when all the required data is passed through the request.

mailTemplateType.description: Only the property `templateData` is used. It provides additional variables to the templating engine.

templateData.description: An associative array that is handed over to the templating engine and can be used as variables in the mail content.

example.messageOfTheDay: An apple a day keeps the doctor away!

mailTemplate.description: The content of the mail as plain text.

contentHtml.description: The content of the mail template in html format.
contentHtml.example: Hello {{ order.customerName }}, this is example mail content, the current date is {{ 'now'|date('d/m/Y') }}

200.description: The rendered simulation result of the mail template parts.

post.description: Generates simulated data for a given event name and renders the provided mail template content against it.

templateParts.description: A map of mail template fields and their template content to render.

eventName.description: The business event name used to generate simulated mail template data.

salesChannelId.description: Identifier of the sales channel that should be used for the simulate request.

strictRendering.description: Enables strict variable rendering and surfaces errors for unknown variables. Defaults to `true`.

200.description: The rendered preview of the mail template.

schema.description: A map of render results keyed by the same field names that were provided in the request's `templateParts` object.

type.description: The type of the render result (either `success` or `error`)

content.description: The rendered content of the mail template field. Also contains the error when rendering this field wasn't possible.

400.description: Invalid request data or invalid eventName

post.description: Fetches the required data needed for the mail template and generates a preview.

mailTemplateId.description: Identifier of the mail template which should be rendered.

salesChannelId.description: Identifier of the sales channel that should be used for the preview request.

includeHeaderFooter.description: Includes the sales channel mail header and footer in `contentHtml` and `contentPlain`. Defaults to `false`.

strictRendering.description: Enables strict variable rendering and enables errors on e.g. unknown variables. Defaults to `false`.

entities.description: A map of entity names and corresponding identifiers that define entities which should be used additionally during rendering.

templateData.description: A map of template data that should be used additionally during rendering. Values in this map override generated or entity-derived template data with the same top-level keys.

200.description: The rendered preview of the mail template.

type.description: The type of the render result (either `success` or `error`)

content.description: The rendered content of the mail template's subject. Also contains the error when rendering this field wasn't possible.

type.description: The type of the render result (either `success` or `error`)

content.description: The rendered content of the mail template's sender name. Also contains the error when rendering this field wasn't possible.

type.description: The type of the render result (either `success` or `error`)

content.description: The rendered content of the mail template in html format. Also contains the error when rendering this field wasn't possible.
content.example:
Hello Mr. Mustermann,
this is example mail content, the current date is 16.03.2026.

type.description: The type of the render result (either `success` or `error`)

content.description: The rendered content of the mail template in plain text format. Also contains the error when rendering this field wasn't possible.
content.example: Hello Mr. Mustermann, this is example mail content, the current date is 16.03.2026.

400.description: Invalid request data or invalid mailTemplateId

post.summary: Fetch the template data and send a mail
post.description: Generates the required data needed for the mail template, renders the mail content and sends it to the customer.

mailTemplateId.description: Identifier of the mail template which should be rendered.

recipients.description: A map of recipients with name and mail address.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

salesChannelId.description: Identifier of the sales channel from which the mail should be send.

contentHtml.description: The content of the mail in HTML format. If passed, this content will be used instead of the content from the mail template.

contentPlain.description: The content of the mail as plain text. If passed, this content will be used instead of the content from the mail template.

subject.description: Subject of the mail. If passed, this subject will be used instead of the subject from the mail template.

senderName.description: Name of the sender. If passed, this sender name will be used instead of the sender name from the mail template.

senderEmail.description: Mail address of the sender. If not set, `core.basicInformation.email` or `core.mailerSettings.senderAddress` will be used from the shop configuration.

mediaIds.description: List of media identifiers which should be attached to the mail.

documentIds.description: List of document identifiers which should be attached to the mail.

binAttachments.description: A list of binary attachments which should be added to the mail.

recipientsBcc.description: A mail address or a map of recipients with name and mail address to be set in BCC.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

recipientsCc.description: A mail address or a map of recipients with name and mail address to be set in CC.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

replyTo.description: A mail address or a map of mail addresses with name to be set in reply-to.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

returnPath.description: A mail address or a map of mail addresses with name to be set in return-path.

additionalProperties.description: Name of the recipient. Can be null to omit the display name.

entities.description: A map of entity names and corresponding identifiers that define entities which should be used additionally during rendering.

templateData.description: A map of template data that should be used additionally during rendering. Values in this map override generated or entity-derived template data with the same top-level keys.

testMode.description: Enables mail test mode. The mail is still sent, but template rendering runs with the renderer's test mode enabled, a fallback `order.deepLinkCode` of `home` is injected when missing, and debug headers such as `X-Shopware-Language-Id` plus available event or sales-channel headers are added to the message.

400.description: Invalid request data or invalid mailTemplateId

post.summary: Fetch the available variables for a business event and an optional parent variable path.
post.description: Fetches the available variables that a business event provides for a certain parent variable path to determine which variables can be used in a mail template.

eventName.description: The business event name that provides the available variables.

parentVariablePath.description: The path to the parent field of which the available variables should be returned. If omitted or empty, the top-level variables of the business event are returned.

200.description: The available variables got fetched successfully.

hasChildren.description: Shows if behind the variable are more nested variables which can be accessed by another request to this endpoint with the updated parent variable path: `<parentVariablePath>.<fieldName>`

schema.description: A collection of the available variables which also show if behind the variables are more nested variables.

400.description: Invalid request data or invalid eventName
